notes: Canonical basic-turn for mistral-vibe: one user prompt, one text-only assistant reply, no tool use, asserting the ready→working→ready arc. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver: the REPL must outlive the daemon's observation window so the working→ready settle is captured. BACKFLOW (live recording): the capture CONFIRMED the presession birth the assessment left provisional — mistral-vibe is a presession adapter. A transient proc-<PID> row is born ready BEFORE the real session_<id> transcript dir appears (the CommandPattern process scanner binds the vibe PID before the lazily-created transcript dir), then reconciles into the real session_<id> under a DIFFERENT session_id. So the birth is modeled as TWO phases (presession_birth → session_birth new_session) with every post-birth phase anchored to the REAL session, templated from the codex sibling — NOT the single-birth shape the assessment assumed. first_turn_end anchors to first_turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the earlier birth ready. OBSERVATIONS: the offline replay golden CANNOT reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (replay stages only transcript.jsonl; model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so model and tokens are UNVERIFIABLE offline — the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens and matches kiro-cli (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false). Pillar verdict (agent=yes, daemon=full) is unchanged.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
User prompt → working on the real session
Text-only assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
